Hezbollah has strongly rejected claims by Benjamin Netanyahu that the Resistance is undermining the ceasefire, warning that his remarks are an attempt to entangle Lebanon in a bilateral arrangement made with Washington without Beirut’s consent.

In a statement, Hezbollah said its attacks on Israeli forces and settlements are a legitimate response to more than 500 ceasefire violations by "Israel" since the truce began, including air, land, and sea strikes that have caused civilian casualties and widespread destruction. The group accused "Israel" of escalating its aggression despite the extension of the ceasefire, calling it evidence of continued disregard for international law.

Hezbollah also criticized the Lebanese authorities, accusing them of failing to secure a halt to Israeli attacks or demand a withdrawal from occupied Lebanese territory and warning that their position risks legitimizing ongoing violations.

The group stressed that continued Israeli strikes, destruction of homes, and occupation of Lebanese land will be met with sustained resistance, asserting its right to respond under international law. It added that it will not rely on "failed diplomacy" or a state unable to protect its people, insisting that the people themselves remain the primary force confronting aggression.

#Lebanon #SouthLebanon #Hezbollah #AlMayadeen
Both sides stressed the importance of regional cooperation in crisis management and continued coordination to support peace efforts, with Qatar reaffirming readiness to play an active mediating role.

#WATCH | The Israeli occupation army continues its systematic campaign of deliberate destruction in South Lebanon; this time in the border town of Debel.

As is undeniably evident in the footage, solar panels that supply electricity to the town were demolished, along with the main water station, cutting off essential services for residents.

The destruction also extended to homes, roads, and olive groves, refuting its narrative of targeting infrastructures related to the Resistance.

#Lebanon #SouthLebanon #AlMayadeen

According to Al Mayadeen’s correspondent in Islamabad, direct negotiations between Iran and the United States remain on hold, as #Iran's key conditions for dialogue have yet to be met by the US.

In this context, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi’s visit to #Oman carries particular weight, given Muscat’s longstanding role as a trusted mediator between Washington and Tehran, our correspondent added. 

Meanwhile, #Pakistan’s role at this stage remains limited and has so far been unable to influence or advance developments, as per our correspondent in Islamabad. 

#IranWar #WarOnIran

A suspect is due in federal court on Monday following a shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner at the Washington Hilton on April 25, attended by US President Donald Trump, Vice President JD Vance, and other officials, according to US Attorney for the District of Columbia Jeanine Pirro.

Pirro confirmed that federal charges have already been filed, including two counts of using a firearm during a crime of violence and one count of assaulting a federal officer with a dangerous weapon. The suspect’s first court appearance will be an arraignment.

She also indicated that the case remains active and that additional charges are expected as investigators continue reviewing evidence.

#WhiteHouse #Trump #JDVance #US
